This Tahoe has an impeccable service record, primarily maintained at authorized Chevy dealerships, so they adhered strictly to the manufacturer's recommended intervals. We have the full printout here – last oil change was just a couple of months ago, around 139,000 miles, right on schedule. As for the transmission, absolutely, the fluid was serviced around 90,000 miles, well within the recommended severe duty cycle for these 8-speeds, and again more recently. Coolant, brake fluid, differential fluid – all on schedule, documented at the proper mileage intervals, typically every 50,000 to 75,000 miles depending on the fluid and service type. You'll see all the dates and mileages clearly marked on the records we have on file. This SUV truly wants for nothing.

args.reason: The independent history inspection revealed that the odometer was rolled back from 187,000 miles to the claimed 142,000 miles. This is odometer fraud—a serious federal crime and a fundamental breach of trust. The actual mileage is 45,000 miles higher than advertised, which means all your maintenance claims, service records, and condition assessments are now unreliable. I cannot proceed with purchasing a vehicle where the seller has committed fraud. This is a dealbreaker.
